diaphragm.role: Drives respiration. In L AIC, the left diaphragm loses its Zone of Apposition (ZOA), flattening and pulling the crura downward.
diaphragm.pattern: L diaphragm descends → L crural pull → lumbar hyperextension bias. Loss of ZOA = loss of respiratory opposition.
diaphragm.correction: Restore L ZOA via 90-90 hip lift with balloon. Exhale to reposition diaphragm dome.

psoas.role: Primary hip flexor and lumbar spine stabilizer. In L AIC, the left psoas shortens, pulling the left ilium into anterior tilt.
psoas.pattern: L psoas shortens → L lumbar lordosis → L IP ER (anterior tilt). Feeds forward into iliacus.
psoas.correction: Inhibit through positioning (90-90). Not directly stretched — repositioned via pelvic correction.

iliacus.role: Hip flexor originating from iliac fossa. Works with psoas to anteriorly tilt the pelvis.
iliacus.pattern: L iliacus pulls ilium forward → L IP ER → anterior pelvic tilt on left.
iliacus.correction: Repositioned via L AF IR activities. Not directly targeted in isolation.

tfl.role: Tensor fasciae latae. Abducts, flexes, and internally rotates the femur. Orientates femoral position.
tfl.pattern: L TFL compensates for femoral orientation in L AIC pattern. Contributes to lateral knee tension.
tfl.correction: Addressed indirectly through femoral repositioning (L AF IR).

vastus_lateralis.role: Largest quadriceps component. Extends the knee with a lateral pull vector.
vastus_lateralis.pattern: Lateral knee tension from TFL transmitted through IT band and vastus lateralis. Knee tracks laterally.
vastus_lateralis.correction: Corrected by restoring femoral IR and addressing proximal chain (hip/pelvis).

biceps_femoris.role: Long head: hip extension and knee flexion. Terminal muscle in L AIC chain.
biceps_femoris.pattern: L biceps femoris (long head) = terminal link. Compensatory ER of tibia on femur.
biceps_femoris.correction: Inhibited as compensator. Corrective hamstring work uses medial hamstrings (semimembranosus/semitendinosus).
